generate_prompt.name: generate_prompt
generate_prompt.description: Generates a structured, token-counted summary of a codebase for LLM analysis. Supports include/exclude, git diffs, and formatting options.

path.description: The source directory or file path to analyze.
path.title: Path

output_file.description: The path where the generated summary file will be saved.
output_file.title: Output File

include.description: A list of glob patterns to explicitly include files (e.g., '*.py', 'src/**/*').
include.title: Include

exclude.description: A list of glob patterns to exclude files (e.g., '*_test.py', 'dist/*').
exclude.title: Exclude

output_format.description: The output format for the summary. Valid options include 'markdown', 'json'.
output_format.title: Output Format

line_numbers.description: Include line numbers in code blocks.
line_numbers.title: Line Numbers

full_directory_tree.description: Display full directory tree including empty directories.
full_directory_tree.title: Full Directory Tree

no_codeblock.description: Omit markdown code block fences around file content.
no_codeblock.title: No Codeblock

absolute_paths.description: Use absolute paths instead of relative paths.
absolute_paths.title: Absolute Paths

encoding.description: The name of the tiktoken encoding to use for token counting (e.g., 'cl100k', 'p50k_base').
encoding.title: Encoding

token_format.description: Determines how token counts are displayed. Valid options are 'format' (human readable) or 'raw' (machine parsable).
token_format.title: Token Format

sort.description: The sorting order for files. Options: 'name_asc', 'name_desc', 'date_asc', 'date_desc'.
sort.title: Sort

template.description: Path to a custom Jinja2 template file to format the output.
template.title: Template

include_git_diff.description: Generate content from git diff instead of full directory.
include_git_diff.title: Include Git Diff

git_diff_branch1.description: The first branch or commit for git diff comparison. Requires git_diff_branch2.
git_diff_branch1.title: Git Diff Branch1

git_diff_branch2.description: The second branch or commit for git diff comparison. Requires git_diff_branch1.
git_diff_branch2.title: Git Diff Branch2

git_log_branch1.description: The first branch or commit for git log comparison. Requires git_log_branch2.
git_log_branch1.title: Git Log Branch1

git_log_branch2.description: The second branch or commit for git log comparison. Requires git_log_branch1.
git_log_branch2.title: Git Log Branch2

message.description: A confirmation message indicating the result of the generation.
message.title: Message

output_file_path.description: The absolute path to the generated prompt summary file.
output_file_path.title: Output File Path

file_size_bytes.description: The size of the generated file in bytes.
file_size_bytes.title: File Size Bytes

server_info.name: server_info
server_info.description: Checks the status of the Code2Prompt server and its CLI dependency. Returns version info and available functions.

outputSchema.description: Standardized server information across all tools.

name.description: The name of the MCP tool server.
name.title: Name

version.description: The version of the tool or its primary dependency.
version.title: Version

status.description: The operational status of the server (e.g., 'active', 'error').
status.title: Status

capabilities.description: A list of functions or features the tool provides.
capabilities.title: Capabilities

dependencies.description: A dictionary of dependencies and their versions or statuses.
dependencies.title: Dependencies

outputSchema.title: ServerInfo

download.name: download
download.description: Downloads an ArXiv paper by ID in various formats ('src', 'pdf', 'tex') or lists its source files.

arxiv_id.description: The unique ArXiv identifier for the paper (e.g., '2301.07041').
arxiv_id.title: Arxiv Id

format.description: The format of the paper to download. Valid options are 'src', 'pdf', or 'tex'.
format.title: Format

output_path.description: Path to save the content. For 'pdf' format: saves as a single file. For 'src' and 'tex' formats: creates a directory with this name and extracts files into it. If empty, defaults to '<arxiv_id>.pdf' for pdf or '<arxiv_id>' for source formats. Use '-' to list file info to stdout instead of saving.
output_path.title: Output Path

outputSchema.description: Result of downloading an ArXiv paper.

message.description: A summary message describing the result of the download operation.
message.title: Message

arxiv_id.description: The ArXiv ID of the paper that was downloaded.
arxiv_id.title: Arxiv Id

format.description: The format of the downloaded content (e.g., 'src', 'pdf', 'tex').
format.title: Format

output_path.description: The path where the content was saved, or '-' if printed to stdout.
output_path.title: Output Path

size_bytes.description: The total size of the downloaded content in bytes.
size_bytes.title: Size Bytes

files.description: A list of file names included in the downloaded archive.
files.title: Files

outputSchema.title: DownloadResult

search.name: search
search.description: Searches ArXiv for papers. Supports advanced syntax (e.g., 'au:Hinton', 'ti:attention'). Use include_fields to limit output for context window management.

query.description: The search query. Supports field prefixes (au, ti, abs, co) and boolean operators (AND, OR, ANDNOT).
query.title: Query

max_results.description: The maximum number of results to return.
max_results.title: Max Results

start.description: The starting index for the search results, used for pagination.
start.title: Start

include_fields.description: Specific fields to include in results. If empty, all fields are included. Available: id, title, authors, summary, published, categories, pdf_url, abs_url.
include_fields.title: Include Fields

max_authors.description: Max authors to return per paper. If more, a summary is added. Set to null for no limit.
max_authors.title: Max Authors

max_summary_len.description: Max summary length (characters). Truncates with '...'. Set to null for no limit.
max_summary_len.title: Max Summary Len

ArxivPaper.description:
ArXiv paper metadata.
Fields may be omitted or truncated depending on the parameters used in the search tool.

id.description: The ArXiv ID of the paper (e.g., '2301.07041').
id.title: Id

title.description: The title of the paper (empty if not fetched).
title.title: Title

authors.description: List of authors' names. May be truncated.
authors.title: Authors

summary.description: Abstract or summary of the paper. May be truncated (empty if not fetched).
summary.title: Summary

published.description: Publication date in YYYY-MM-DD format (empty if not fetched).
published.title: Published

pdf_url.description: Direct URL to download the PDF version (empty if not available).
pdf_url.title: Pdf Url

abs_url.description: URL to the ArXiv abstract page (empty if not available).
abs_url.title: Abs Url

ArxivPaper.title: ArxivPaper

server_info.name: server_info
server_info.description: Checks ArXiv tool status and lists available functions. Use this to discover server capabilities.

outputSchema.description: Standardized server information across all tools.

name.description: The name of the MCP tool server.
name.title: Name

version.description: The version of the tool or its primary dependency.
version.title: Version

status.description: The operational status of the server (e.g., 'active', 'error').
status.title: Status

capabilities.description: A list of functions or features the tool provides.
capabilities.title: Capabilities

dependencies.description: A dictionary of dependencies and their versions or statuses.
dependencies.title: Dependencies

outputSchema.title: ServerInfo

read.name: read
read.description: Read calendar events. Get single event by ID (returns singleton list), or search/list events in date range. Use calendar://list resource to discover available calendar IDs.

event_id.description: If provided, get single event by ID (returns singleton list). Cannot use with calendar_id='all'.
event_id.title: Event Id

calendar_id.description: ID or name of the calendar. Use 'all' to search all calendars (only for search, not get-by-id).
calendar_id.title: Calendar Id

search_text.description: Text to search for. If empty, lists all events in the date range.
search_text.title: Search Text

start_date.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD) for search. Defaults to today.
start_date.title: Start Date

end_date.description: End date (YYYY-MM-DD) for search. Defaults to 7 days from start.
end_date.title: End Date

max_results.description: Maximum events to return per calendar.
max_results.title: Max Results

search_fields.description: Client-side filter fields (e.g., 'summary', 'description'). None=API search only, []=search all fields.
search_fields.title: Search Fields

match_all_terms.description: If True (AND), all words must match. If False (OR), any can match.
match_all_terms.title: Match All Terms

email.description: The email address of the attendee.
email.title: Email

id.description: The unique identifier for the event.
id.title: Id

summary.description: The title or summary of the event.
summary.title: Summary

description.description: A detailed description or notes for the event.
description.title: Description

location.description: The physical location or meeting link for the event.
location.title: Location

start.description: The start time of the event, including timezone.

end.description: The end time of the event, including timezone.

attendees.description: A list of people attending the event.
attendees.title: Attendees

calendar_name.description: The name of the calendar this event belongs to.
calendar_name.title: Calendar Name

created.description: The creation time of the event as an ISO 8601 string.
created.title: Created

updated.description: The last modification time of the event as an ISO 8601 string.
updated.title: Updated

dateTime.description: The timestamp for timed events in RFC3339 format (e.g., '2023-12-25T10:00:00Z').
dateTime.title: Datetime

date.description: The date for all-day events in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2023-12-25').
date.title: Date

create.name: create
create.description: Create a new calendar event. Supports natural language datetimes (e.g., 'tomorrow at 2pm') and mixed timezones.

summary.description: The title or summary for the new event.
summary.title: Summary

start_datetime.description: The start time of the event. Supports natural language (e.g., 'tomorrow at 2pm').
start_datetime.title: Start Datetime

end_datetime.description: The end time of the event. Supports natural language (e.g., 'in 3 hours').
end_datetime.title: End Datetime

description.description: A detailed description or notes for the event.
description.title: Description

location.description: The physical location or meeting link for the event.
location.title: Location

calendar_id.description: The ID or name of the calendar to add the event to. Use calendar://list resource to discover options. Required parameter - no default.
calendar_id.title: Calendar Id

start_timezone.description: Explicit IANA timezone for the start time (e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'). Overrides calendar's default.
start_timezone.title: Start Timezone

end_timezone.description: Explicit IANA timezone for the end time. Essential for events spanning timezones, like flights.
end_timezone.title: End Timezone

attendees.description: A list of attendee email addresses to invite to the event.
attendees.title: Attendees

outputSchema.description: Result of creating a calendar event.

status.description: The status of the event creation (e.g., 'confirmed', 'tentative').
status.title: Status

event_id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the newly created event.
event_id.title: Event Id

title.description: The title of the created event.
title.title: Title

time.description: A human-readable summary of when the event occurs.
time.title: Time

calendar.description: The name or ID of the calendar where the event was created.
calendar.title: Calendar

attendees.description: A list of attendee email addresses for the event.
attendees.title: Attendees

outputSchema.title: CreatedEventResult

update.name: update
update.description: Update or move a calendar event. If destination_calendar_id provided, moves event (may change event ID). Otherwise updates event properties.

event_id.description: The unique identifier of the event to update or move.
event_id.title: Event Id

calendar_id.description: The calendar where the event is located. Defaults to primary.
calendar_id.title: Calendar Id

destination_calendar_id.description: If provided, move event to this calendar instead of updating. Cannot combine with update fields.
destination_calendar_id.title: Destination Calendar Id

start_datetime.description: New start time. Supports natural language. None=no change.
start_datetime.title: Start Datetime

end_datetime.description: New end time. Supports natural language. None=no change.
end_datetime.title: End Datetime

start_timezone.description: New IANA timezone for start. If empty, preserves existing.
start_timezone.title: Start Timezone

end_timezone.description: New IANA timezone for end. If empty, preserves existing.
end_timezone.title: End Timezone

normalize_timezone.description: Fix timezone inconsistencies (UTC time with non-UTC label).
normalize_timezone.title: Normalize Timezone

outputSchema.description: Result of a successful event update or move operation.

event_id.description: The unique identifier of the updated event.
event_id.title: Event Id

new_event_id.description: For move operations: the new event ID (may differ from original). Empty for updates.
new_event_id.title: New Event Id

html_link.description: A direct link to the event in the Google Calendar UI.
html_link.title: Html Link

updated_fields.description: A list of the fields that were modified in this update operation.
updated_fields.title: Updated Fields

outputSchema.title: UpdateEventResult

delete.name: delete
delete.description: Delete a calendar event permanently. WARNING: Irreversible.

event_id.description: The unique identifier of the event to delete.
event_id.title: Event Id

calendar_id.description: The calendar where the event is located. Defaults to primary.
calendar_id.title: Calendar Id

get_directions.name: get_directions
get_directions.description: Gets directions between an origin and destination, supporting multiple travel modes, waypoints, and route preferences. For transit, supports specific transport modes and routing preferences.

origin.description: The starting address, place name, or coordinates (e.g., '1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA').
origin.title: Origin

destination.description: The ending address, place name, or coordinates (e.g., 'San Francisco, CA').
destination.title: Destination

mode.description: The mode of transport to use for the directions.
mode.title: Mode

departure_time.description: The desired departure time. Supports natural language ('17:00', '5pm', 'tomorrow 5pm'), relative times ('in 2 hours'), or ISO 8601 formats. Times are interpreted as UK local time unless explicitly specified. Cannot be used with arrival_time.
departure_time.title: Departure Time

arrival_time.description: The desired arrival time. Supports natural language ('17:00', '5pm', 'tomorrow 5pm'), relative times ('in 2 hours'), or ISO 8601 formats. Times are interpreted as UK local time unless explicitly specified. Cannot be used with departure_time.
arrival_time.title: Arrival Time

user_timezone.description: The timezone to use for interpreting departure/arrival times when not explicitly specified (e.g., 'Europe/London', 'America/New_York'). Defaults to UK timezone.
user_timezone.title: User Timezone

avoid.description: A list of route features to avoid (e.g., 'tolls', 'highways'). Only for 'driving' mode.
avoid.title: Avoid

alternatives.description: If True, requests that alternative routes be provided in the response.
alternatives.title: Alternatives

waypoints.description: A list of addresses or coordinates to route through between the origin and destination.
waypoints.title: Waypoints

transit_mode.description: Preferred modes of public transit. Only for 'transit' mode.
transit_mode.title: Transit Mode

transit_routing_preference.description: Specifies preferences for transit routes, such as fewer transfers or less walking. Only for 'transit' mode.
transit_routing_preference.title: Transit Routing Preference

DirectionLeg.description: A leg of a route (origin to destination or waypoint).

distance.description: The total distance for this leg of the journey.
distance.title: Distance

duration.description: The estimated total time for this leg of the journey.
duration.title: Duration

start_address.description: The human-readable address where this leg begins.
start_address.title: Start Address

end_address.description: The human-readable address where this leg ends.
end_address.title: End Address

steps.description: The individual navigation steps that make up this leg.
steps.title: Steps

DirectionLeg.title: DirectionLeg

DirectionRoute.description: A complete route with all legs and steps.

summary.description: A short textual description of the route (e.g., 'via I-95 N').
summary.title: Summary

legs.description: The individual legs that make up this complete route.
legs.title: Legs

distance.description: The total distance for the entire route.
distance.title: Distance

duration.description: The estimated total time for the entire route.
duration.title: Duration

polyline.description: An encoded polyline representation of the route path.
polyline.title: Polyline

warnings.description: Any warnings about the route (e.g., tolls, traffic).
warnings.title: Warnings

DirectionRoute.title: DirectionRoute

DirectionStep.description: A single step in a route.

instruction.description: Human-readable navigation instruction for this step.
instruction.title: Instruction

distance.description: The distance for this step (e.g., '0.5 km', '500 ft').
distance.title: Distance

duration.description: The estimated time for this step (e.g., '5 mins', '2 hours').
duration.title: Duration

start_location.description: The latitude and longitude coordinates where this step begins.
start_location.title: Start Location

end_location.description: The latitude and longitude coordinates where this step ends.
end_location.title: End Location

travel_mode.description: The mode of transport for this step (e.g., 'WALKING', 'DRIVING', 'TRANSIT').
travel_mode.title: Travel Mode

transit_details.description: Additional details if this step involves public transit.

DirectionStep.title: DirectionStep

departure_time.description: The scheduled departure time for this transit step in UK local time (BST/GMT with proper offset).
departure_time.title: Departure Time

arrival_time.description: The scheduled arrival time for this transit step in UK local time (BST/GMT with proper offset).
arrival_time.title: Arrival Time

line_name.description: The full name of the transit line (e.g., 'Red Line', 'Route 101').
line_name.title: Line Name

line_short_name.description: The short name or number of the transit line.
line_short_name.title: Line Short Name

headsign.description: The destination sign displayed on the transit vehicle.
headsign.title: Headsign

num_stops.description: The number of stops between boarding and alighting.
num_stops.title: Num Stops

routes.description: A list of possible routes from origin to destination.
routes.title: Routes

status.description: The status of the API request (e.g., 'OK', 'ZERO_RESULTS').
status.title: Status

origin.description: The address or coordinates of the starting point.
origin.title: Origin

destination.description: The address or coordinates of the ending point.
destination.title: Destination

mode.description: The travel mode used for the directions (e.g., 'driving', 'transit').
mode.title: Mode

departure_time.description: The requested departure time as an ISO 8601 string, if provided.
departure_time.title: Departure Time

maps_url.description: A direct URL to Google Maps with the requested route.
maps_url.title: Maps Url

server_info.name: server_info
server_info.description: Get Google Maps Tool server information and capabilities.

outputSchema.description: Standardized server information across all tools.

name.description: The name of the MCP tool server.
name.title: Name

version.description: The version of the tool or its primary dependency.
version.title: Version

status.description: The operational status of the server (e.g., 'active', 'error').
status.title: Status

capabilities.description: A list of functions or features the tool provides.
capabilities.title: Capabilities

dependencies.description: A dictionary of dependencies and their versions or statuses.
dependencies.title: Dependencies

outputSchema.title: ServerInfo

send.name: send
send.description: Send an email via Mutt. Supports compose (new), reply, and forward modes. For replying, prefer using 'reply' mode with a message_id to maintain thread context - the full conversation thread will be included in quotes. All emails open in Mutt for user sign-off before sending.

to.description: Recipient email address. Required for compose/forward, auto-populated for reply.
to.title: To

subject.description: The subject line of the email.
subject.title: Subject

body.description: Email body text. For reply/forward, added above quoted/forwarded content.
body.title: Body

cc.description: Email address for the 'Cc' (carbon copy) field.
cc.title: Cc

bcc.description: Email address for the 'Bcc' (blind carbon copy) field.
bcc.title: Bcc

attachments.description: A list of local file paths to attach to the email.
attachments.title: Attachments

message_id.description: For reply/forward: the notmuch message ID of the email to reply to or forward.
message_id.title: Message Id

reply_all.description: For reply mode: if True, reply to all recipients (To and Cc).
reply_all.title: Reply All

thread_context.description: For reply mode: number of previous thread messages to include (0 to disable, -1 for all).
thread_context.title: Thread Context

status.description: The outcome status of the operation.
status.title: Status

message.description: Human-readable description of the operation result.
message.title: Message

data.description: Additional structured data related to the operation.
data.title: Data

contacts.name: contacts
contacts.description: Manage Mutt address book contacts. Actions: 'find' (search), 'add' (create), 'remove' (delete). Find uses fuzzy matching on alias/name/email.

query.description: For 'find': search term. For 'add'/'remove': the alias.
query.title: Query

config_file.description: Optional path to mutt config file.
config_file.title: Config File

search.name: search
search.description: Search emails using notmuch query language. Supports sender, subject, date ranges, tags, attachments, and body content filtering with boolean operators.

query.description: A valid notmuch search query. Examples: 'from:boss', 'tag:inbox and date:2024-01-01..', 'subject:"Project X"'.
query.title: Query

limit.description: The maximum number of message IDs to return.
limit.title: Limit

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.
offset.title: Offset

include_excluded.description: Include emails with excluded tags (spam, deleted) that are normally hidden.
include_excluded.title: Include Excluded

SearchResult.description: Structured search result for a single email.

id.description: The unique message ID of the email.
id.title: Id

subject.description: The subject line of the email.
subject.title: Subject

from_address.description: The sender's email address and name.
from_address.title: From Address

to_address.description: The primary recipient's email address (empty if not available).
to_address.title: To Address

date.description: The date the email was sent (empty if not available).
date.title: Date

SearchResult.title: SearchResult

show.name: show
show.description: Display email content with optimized token efficiency. Uses advanced libraries (email-reply-parser, selectolax, inscriptis) for clean text extraction with minimal token usage. Supports progressive rendering modes to control output verbosity. Optionally saves body and attachments to files.

query.description: A notmuch query to select the email(s) to display. Typically an 'id:<message-id>' query for a single email.
query.title: Query

mode.description: Rendering mode: 'headers' (metadata only), 'summary' (first 2000 chars), or 'full' (complete optimized content)
mode.title: Mode

limit.description: Maximum number of emails to return (helps prevent token overflow). If None, returns all emails.
limit.title: Limit

include_excluded.description: Include emails with excluded tags (spam, deleted) that are normally hidden by notmuch.
include_excluded.title: Include Excluded

save_attachments_to.description: Directory to save email body and attachments to. Body saved as .txt/.html, attachments saved with original filenames. Paths returned in saved_files field.
save_attachments_to.title: Save Attachments To

EmailContent.description: Structured representation of a single email's content.

id.description: The unique message ID of the email.
id.title: Id

subject.description: The subject line of the email.
subject.title: Subject

from_address.description: The sender's email address and name.
from_address.title: From Address

to_address.description: The primary recipient's email address and name.
to_address.title: To Address

date.description: The date the email was sent, in a human-readable format.
date.title: Date

tags.description: A list of notmuch tags associated with the email.
tags.title: Tags

body_markdown.description: The body of the email, converted to Markdown for best LLM comprehension. Preserves lists, tables, links, and formatting.
body_markdown.title: Body Markdown

body_format.description: The original format of the body ('html' or 'text'). Indicates if the markdown was converted or is original plain text.
body_format.title: Body Format

attachments.description: A list of filenames for any attachments in the email.
attachments.title: Attachments

saved_files.description: Paths to saved files when save_attachments_to is used (body + attachments).
saved_files.title: Saved Files

EmailContent.title: EmailContent

tag.name: tag
tag.description: Add or remove tags from emails by message ID. Primary method for organizing emails in notmuch.

message_id.description: The notmuch message ID of the email to modify.
message_id.title: Message Id

add_tags.description: A list of tags to add to the email.
add_tags.title: Add Tags

remove_tags.description: A list of tags to remove from the email.
remove_tags.title: Remove Tags

message_id.description: The notmuch message ID that was tagged.
message_id.title: Message Id

added_tags.description: A list of tags that were added to the message.
added_tags.title: Added Tags

removed_tags.description: A list of tags that were removed from the message.
removed_tags.title: Removed Tags

move.name: move
move.description: Moves emails to an existing maildir folder (e.g., 'Trash', 'Archive'). Automatically finds the correct folder within the same email account (e.g., 'Hermes/Archive' for emails from 'Hermes/INBOX'). Will not create new folders - only moves to existing ones.

message_ids.description: A list of notmuch message IDs for the emails to be moved.
message_ids.title: Message Ids

destination_folder.description: The destination maildir folder name (e.g., 'Trash', 'Archive'). Must be an existing folder. The tool will find the appropriate folder within the same email account.
destination_folder.title: Destination Folder

outputSchema.description: Result of a successful email move operation.

message_ids.description: The list of message IDs that were targeted for moving.
message_ids.title: Message Ids

destination_folder.description: The maildir folder the emails were moved to.
destination_folder.title: Destination Folder

moved_files_count.description: The number of email files successfully moved.
moved_files_count.title: Moved Files Count

status.description: A summary of the move operation.
status.title: Status

outputSchema.title: MoveResult

sync.name: sync
sync.description: Unified email synchronization tool. Modes: 'full' (complete sync), 'quick' (new messages only), 'preview' (dry-run), 'status' (validate config), 'info' (show repo details). Use 'folders' param to sync specific folders only.

mode.description: Sync mode: 'full' (complete), 'quick' (fast, new only), 'preview' (dry-run), 'status' (validate config), 'info' (repo details).
mode.title: Mode

account.description: Optional account name to sync. If omitted, all accounts are synced.
account.title: Account

folders.description: Comma-separated folder names to sync (e.g., 'INBOX,Sent'). If omitted, all folders are synced.
folders.title: Folders

config_file.description: Optional path to offlineimap config. Defaults to ~/.offlineimaprc.
config_file.title: Config File

timeout_seconds.description: Timeout in seconds (0 uses mode defaults: full=300, quick/preview=180, status=60, info=120).
timeout_seconds.title: Timeout Seconds

status.description: The outcome status of the operation.
status.title: Status

message.description: Human-readable description of the operation result.
message.title: Message

data.description: Additional structured data related to the operation.
data.title: Data

list.name: list
list.description: List email-related items. Types: 'tags' (notmuch tags), 'folders' (maildir folders), 'accounts' (msmtp send accounts).
